compiler_trust_notes: Olova now carries an accession-backed direct age override from the GPL14550 long-window expression trajectory.
The direct age contract uses fibroblast anchors on one side and a combined ESC-plus-clean-iPSC pluripotent anchor on the other side.
The live override is the mean of the day20 and day28 trajectory projections, which earns a family-local long-window age score without pretending the family resolves late identity or risk.

The GSE54848 methylation arm is present and important, but this tranche does not yet pin a clock-exact CpG implementation inside the repo, so methylation remains an explicit future tightening path rather than a silent dependency.

Identity and risk remain manual benchmark judgments because the family is bulk-only and the source itself warns that safe rejuvenation boundaries are not precisely resolved by the bulk expression surface.

matrix_source_locators: HDF fibroblast anchors: HDF1, HDF3, HDF-1388, HDFa
Pluripotent anchor: H1, H9, KhES1, KhES3, and 253G1-KLF4 dox0 T60
OSKM day20 and day28 bulk trajectory groups
matrix_support_summaries: The accession-backed Olova trajectory moves monotonically from fibroblast baseline toward the pluripotent anchor through day28, with projections of 0.224, 0.471, 0.510, 0.566, 0.857, and 0.929 from day3 to day28.
The direct Olova age override uses the mean of the day20 and day28 projections (0.893), which now overwrites age_signal_score for the long-window maturation condition.
Identity and risk remain manual because this family is bulk-only and the repo does not yet pin a clock-exact methylation contract or a single-cell late-identity boundary for Olova in this tranche.
